request.question: Which actress that was a member of South Korean girl group is co-staring in Unexpected Love?

title: Krystal Jung
Black Pearl (K-Pop Group)
Jewelry (band)
Unexpected Love (2017 film)
Kiss (Dara song)
Kim Chung-ha
D.Holic
Kwon So-hyun
GP Basic
Lina (South Korean singer)
sentences: Chrystal Soo Jung (born October 24, 1994), better known by the mononym Krystal, is an American singer and actress based in South Korea.
She debuted in 2009 as a member of South Korean girl group f(x) and has further participated in S.M. Entertainment's project group S.M.
The Ballad.
Aside from group activities, she has also participated in various television dramas such as "" (2011–12), "The Heirs" (2013), "My Lovely Girl" (2014), and "The Bride of Habaek" (2017).
Black Pearl (Hangul: 블랙펄) was a South Korean girl group official made up of Oh Nami, Hwl Eun, Young Joo, and Mikka.
When the group debuted in 2007 they marketed as the new girl group to dominate the girl group charts in the following years of 2007 and 2008 alongside some artist Wonder Girls, and Kara.
They were also known to follow the steps of SG Wannabe, SeeYa, and M2M.
In 2009 Mikka left due to personal reasons.
New member Jung Min was added by 2010 or 2011.
As for the 2010 and therefore the group only consisted of the following 4 until their disbandment in 2012.
Jewelry (Korean: 쥬얼리 ) was a South Korean girl group formed in 2001 by Star Empire Entertainment.
The group was the longest-running Korean girl group (with more than 2 members) until their disbandment in 2015, surpassing super-group Baby V.O.X.
Unexpected Love (also anown as: "Flying Kite" / "Bi Zui!
Ai Ba" / "Biyeon") is an upcoming Chinese-South Korean romantic comedy film directed by Si-Heup Seong and starring Zhang Yixing and Krystal Jung.
"Kiss" (Korean: 입맞춤) is a dance single performed by South Korean idol singer Sandara Park, a visual and member of the girl group 2NE1.
The song was used as part of a promotional campaign for Cass Beer.
It features the vocals of her co-member, CL of South Korean girl group 2NE1.
The song was released as her first official single in South Korea and was later included in the album "To Anyone".
Kim Chung-ha (born Kim Chan-mi on February 9, 1996), better known by the mononym Chungha, is a South Korean singer.
She is best known for finishing fourth in Mnet's girl group survival show "Produce 101" and as a former member of the now disbanded South Korean girl group I.O.I.
D.Holic (Hangul: 디홀릭) was a South Korean girl group formed by Star Road Entertainment (formerly H.Mate Entertainment) in 2014 with five members.
Nine left the group in August 2015, due to personal reasons, and was replaced by new member, Hwajung.
In July 2016, it was revealed through teasers that Danbee and Duri had decided to leave the group, new member, EJ, was added to the line-up.
In February 2017, it was confirmed through a performance that members Hami and Hwajung had departed from the group.
They were temporary replaced with new members, Nayoung and Youjin, although they never officially made their debut.
In July 2017, EJ announced that she would be leaving the group to pursue a modelling career.
The group informally disbanded after the departure of all but one member, with the aim of re-debuting the remaining member, Rena, into a new girl group within a year.
The group has released one mini-album: "Chewy" (2015) and three single albums: "D.Holic Dark With Dignity" (2014), "Murphy & Sally" (2015), and "Color Me Rad" (2016).
Kwon So-hyun (born August 30, 1994), is a South Korean singer and actress.
She was known as a member of the South Korean girl group 4Minute, under Cube Entertainment.
She is also a former member of the South Korean girl group, Orange.
Before June 15, 2016 (end of her contract with Cube), Sohyun left 4Minute and the record label along with members Nam Ji-hyun, Heo Ga-yoon, and Jeon Ji-yoon.
GP Basic (Korean: 지피 베이직 ) was a South Korean girl group who debuted on August 15, 2010.
At the time of their debut, they were considered to be the "youngest" South Korean girl group with an average age of below 13.5.
The age of their youngest member, Janey, caused controversy followed by the debut of a much younger girl group, G-story, who were all below 10 years old.
This led to new broadcasting regulations in South Korea.
Lee Ji-yeon (born February 18, 1984), better known by her stage name Lina is a South Korean singer and musical actress.
She debuted in 2002 as a member of South Korean R&B duo Isak N Jiyeon and disbanded in 2004.
She later re-debut as a member of South Korean girl group The Grace in 2005 and the group currently being inactive.
She currently active as a musical actress.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Task: Compress the grounded first-hop evidence into a single concise, retrieval-oriented intermediate state to guide the next-hop retrieval. Do not produce the final answer or perform any new retrieval here.

Required content of `summary` (single field):
- A short header identifying the primary topic(s) found in the passages (1 line).
- A concise enumeration of the concrete facts supported verbatim by the provided passages: list entity mentions and directly supported factual spans (dates, years, titles, roles). Use very short quoted fragments (<= ~15 words) only when those words appear verbatim in the passages. Keep to facts present in the passages; do not add or infer new facts.
- Explicitly expose any ambiguity or missing information that blocks answering: if multiple candidate entities or page variants appear, enumerate those candidates and label the name as ambiguous; if a required target fact is missing or ambiguous, label it as the missing fact and identify the bridge entity that connects this hop to the next.
- Prioritized next-hop retrieval directives (1–4 items): concrete page titles or disambiguation pages to fetch, explicit article sections to inspect (e.g., lede/infobox, "Career", "Publication", "History"), or tight search keywords. Order them by priority and be specific (include possible disambiguation terms or section names).

Formatting and style constraints:
- Keep the entire `summary` concise: a short header plus 1–4 brief sentences or 2–6 short bullet lines in total.
- Preserve the habit of extracting and citing concrete facts verbatim from the passages (names, dates, passage labels).
- Do not output a final answer, comparison, or any numeric fact that purports to resolve the question here. Even if the passages fully and unambiguously contain all required facts, present that evidence and explicitly state "no additional retrieval needed," but defer producing any final answer to a downstream component.
- If a name or term has multiple distinct variants in the passages, do not pick one without disambiguating evidence; enumerate candidates instead.
- Do not invent or add facts not present in the supplied passages. Avoid long verbatim quotations; prefer short extracts (<= ~15 words) or paraphrase.

Output expectations: produce exactly one field `summary` containing the concise, retrieval-oriented summary that meets the above requirements.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact, retrieval-focused second-hop query in the field `query`. Use only the provided `question` and `summary_1` as inputs. Do not include any explanation, answer, fallback text, lists, or additional fields — the output must be a single one-line query string.

Decision templates (choose the single template that matches the missing evidence implied by summary_1):
- Connector between two named items: emit a short interrogative naming both items and requesting the linking evidence (e.g., artist album recording) in one line. Retain the visible item tokens from the inputs.
- Metadata for a single anchor title: emit a compact keyword-style query consisting of the anchor plus brief attribute keywords (for example: artist genre).
- Temporal or aggregate fact: include the year or season token plus the entity type and a short cue such as season count.

Strict formatting rules:
- Emit exactly one line and exactly one string in `query` containing only the query text.
- Keep the query compact (aim for ≤ 10–12 words/tokens) and free of punctuation or explanatory clauses.
- Preserve visible tokens from `question` and `summary_1` (title words, years, or entity names) to anchor retrieval.
- Do not answer, conclude, enumerate candidates, assert hidden facts, or include user-facing instructions.

Produce the `query` field now.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Instructions (must follow exactly):
- Emit a single field named `summary` containing only 1–3 concise sentences that integrate the first-hop `context` and the second-hop `passages` into a grounded post-retrieval state.
- If the `context` already contains a concise answer sentence, keep that sentence as the first sentence of `summary` unless the `passages` clearly contradict it; append supporting sentence(s) after it rather than replacing it.
- Preserve the primary answer name(s) exactly as they appear in the inputs (context or passages) unless those names clearly conflict with stronger passage content.
- When one or more `passages` supply short biographical, membership, partnership, or corroborating facts about a named entity from the `context`, append at most one brief supporting sentence per relevant named person. Each such supporting sentence should:
  - begin with the entity's canonical name as shown in the passages (if available),
  - paraphrase up to one or two key attributes present in the passages (examples: nationality, award/winner status, current affiliation/team, partner identity, founding/formation fact, birth date), and
  - avoid long verbatim copying (prefer paraphrase; use short quotes only when a brief exact phrase from a passage is necessary for clarity).
- If the question requires naming both members of a partnership or collaborator pair, name both people in the summary and, if passages provide partner-specific attributes, include one brief attribute for each as separate short clauses or sentences if needed.
- Do not invent facts not present in the provided passages. If passages conflict on a specific attribute, do not synthesize a new reconciled fact; instead prefer the passage that most directly addresses the question or omit the disputed attribute (or state necessary ambiguity only when needed to avoid a false claim).
- Keep the `summary` compact and focused: avoid extraneous biography, discography/chart data, or unrelated commentary. When `context` is absent or is not a concise answer, produce a 1–2 sentence integrated summary naming the relevant entity(ies) and including at most one brief paraphrased attribute drawn from the passages.

Output requirement: exactly one JSON field `summary` whose value is the integrated 1–3 sentence summary.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Instructions:
- Use only `summary_1` and `summary_2` as the evidential basis for the final decision; do not add new facts or external information.
- First determine whether the question is a binary (yes/no) proposition and whether both `summary_1` and `summary_2` clearly and unambiguously support the same binary outcome.
- If BOTH summaries unambiguously assert the same binary conclusion, emit exactly one lowercase token as the entire content of `answer`: use "yes" for an affirmative conclusion and "no" for a negative conclusion. The `answer` field must contain that single token and nothing else (no punctuation, no extra whitespace, no explanation).
- Prefer `summary_2` as the decisive statement when it clarifies or restates `summary_1`, but still require that both summaries support the same binary judgment before emitting the single-token `answer`.
- If the summaries conflict, if either summary hedges, is ambiguous, silent about the binary relation, or otherwise fails to clearly agree, do NOT apply the single-token rule; instead produce the module's normal fallback/unresolved output for `answer`.
- Always remain truthful to the visible summaries and do not alter their factual conclusions.

response.hop1_query: Which actress that was a member of South Korean girl group is co-staring in Unexpected Love?
hop1_docs: Ryu Hyo-young | Ryu Hyoyoung (born April 22, 1993), better known by the mononym Hyoyoung, is a South Korean actress, model, and rapper.  She was a member of the South Korean co-ed group Coed School and the girl group F-ve Dolls.
Lee Soomi | Lee Soomi (; born March 3, 1989) is a South Korean singer, dancer, rapper, and actress.  She is a former member of girl groups SeeYa, F-ve Dolls, and coed group Coed School.

Shoo (singer) | Yoo Soo-young or Shū Kunimitsu (born 23 October 1981), better known as Shoo, is a South Korean singer and musical actress.  She is best known as a member of South Korean girl group S.E.S..  In 2010, she released her debut solo extended play, "Devote One's Love."

Gain (singer) | Son Ga-in (born September 20, 1987), better known mononymously as Gain, is a South Korean singer, actress and entertainer.  She is best known as a member of the South Korean girl group Brown Eyed Girls and for her appearances alongside Jo Kwon from 2AM for TV shows "We Got Married" and "All My Love".  As a solo artist she has released six EPs.

Lina (South Korean singer) | Lee Ji-yeon (born February 18, 1984), better known by her stage name Lina is a South Korean singer and musical actress.  She debuted in 2002 as a member of South Korean R&B duo Isak N Jiyeon and disbanded in 2004.  She later re-debut as a member of South Korean girl group The Grace in 2005 and the group currently being inactive.  She currently active as a musical actress.

Yura (South Korean singer) | Kim Ah-young (born November 6, 1992), better known by her stage name Yura, is a South Korean singer and actress.  She is best known as a member of the South Korean girl group Girl's Day.

Krystal Jung | Chrystal Soo Jung (born October 24, 1994), better known by the mononym Krystal, is an American singer and actress based in South Korea.  She debuted in 2009 as a member of South Korean girl group f(x) and has further participated in S.M. Entertainment's project group S.M.  The Ballad.  Aside from group activities, she has also participated in various television dramas such as "" (2011–12), "The Heirs" (2013), "My Lovely Girl" (2014), and "The Bride of Habaek" (2017).
response.summary_1:
Primary topic: Candidate South Korean girl‑group actresses (possible co‑stars).

- Ryu Hyoyoung — "(born April 22, 1993)"; "She was a member of ... Coed School and the girl group F-ve Dolls."
- Lee Soomi — "(born March 3, 1989)"; "former member of girl groups SeeYa, F-ve Dolls, and coed group Coed School."
- Shoo (Yoo Soo‑young) — "(born 23 October 1981)"; "member of South Korean girl group S.E.S."
- Gain (Son Ga‑in) — "(born September 20, 1987)"; "member of the South Korean girl group Brown Eyed Girls."
- Lina (Lee Ji‑yeon) — "(born February 18, 1984)"; "member of South Korean girl group The Grace."
- Yura (Kim Ah‑young) — "(born November 6, 1992)"; "member of the South Korean girl group Girl's Day."
- Krystal Jung — "(born October 24, 1994)"; "debuted in 2009 as a member of South Korean girl group f(x)."

Missing / ambiguous facts blocking the answer:
- None of the passages state any connection between these actresses and "Unexpected Love." Missing fact: "Which of the above actresses 'co-starred in Unexpected Love'?" — bridge entity: the film/TV work "Unexpected Love" and its cast list.

Prioritized next-hop retrievals:
1. Fetch "Unexpected Love" (disambiguation) and "Unexpected Love (film)" or "Unexpected Love (TV series)" — inspect lead/infobox and "Cast" section for cast names.
2. Fetch each candidate's article pages (Ryu Hyoyoung; Lee Soomi; Shoo; Gain (Son Ga‑in); Lina (Lee Ji‑yeon); Yura (Kim Ah‑young); Krystal Jung) — inspect "Filmography", "Television", or "Career" sections for credits mentioning "Unexpected Love."
3. Search query if needed: "Unexpected Love cast 'Unexpected Love' cast list" and "Unexpected Love 'co‑star' 'Unexpected Love' [actress name]" for direct match.
hop2_docs: Unexpected Love | Unexpected Love () is a 2014 Chinese romance film directed by Bowie Lau and starring Du Chun.  It was released on 14 February 2014.

Unexpected Love (2017 film) | Unexpected Love (also anown as: "Flying Kite" / "Bi Zui!  Ai Ba" / "Biyeon") is an upcoming Chinese-South Korean romantic comedy film directed by Si-Heup Seong and starring Zhang Yixing and Krystal Jung.

Unexpected (Lumidee album) | Unexpected is the second studio album by American hip-hop and R&B recording artist Lumidee, released by TVT Records on April 17, 2007 in the United States and on June 21, 2007 in the United Kingdom.  The album was produced primarily by J. Marty with contributions made by Scott Storch, T.C. Love, Lenky, Ron Browz, Wyclef Jean, Jerry Duplessis and Red Spyda.

To Love Somebody (album) | To Love Somebody is an album by Jazz singer-songwriter/pianist Nina Simone.  It was released as quickly as possible to prolong the unexpected success of "'Nuff Said! " The title is taken from the Bee Gees song "To Love Somebody", her cover of the song became her second British hit single after "Ain't Got No-I Got Life".

Hello Heartbreak | "Hello Heartbreak" is a dance-pop song recorded by American singer-songwriter Michelle Williams.  It was co-written by Williams, James Scheffer, Travis Jones and Rico Love, and produced by Alex da Kid and Jay Wes for Williams' third studio album "Unexpected" (2008).  The song was set to be released as the album's third single and as the first official UK single from the re-release of "Unexpected", but plans were shelved.

Doron Ofir | Doron Ofir is a casting director and president of Doron Ofir Casting, a division of Popular Productions, Inc.  He has worked on various reality television programs, including MTV's "Jersey Shore" and "A Shot at Love with Tila Tequila", HGTV's "Design Star", NBC's "Nashville Star", Fox's "More to Love", E!'s "Rich Kids of Beverly Hills", among others.  He has been credited as Casting Director, Executive Producer, or Executive Head of Casting on over 100 television productions.

True Love Cast Out All Evil | True Love Cast Out All Evil is the first album of new material released by Roky Erickson in 14 years.  Okkervil River is the backing band on the album.  The album was released by Anti- in America and by Chemikal Underground in Europe.
hop2_titles: True Love Cast Out All Evil
response.summary_2: Krystal Jung is co-starring in Unexpected Love. Krystal Jung debuted in 2009 as a member of the South Korean girl group f(x).
